findings.evidence: Existing API is in `code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/EffectiveBounds/Regulator.lean:47` (`regulator_eq_one_of_rank_eq_zero`), `:56` (`one_le_regulator_of_rank_eq_zero`), `:61` (`rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one`), and `:66` (`rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_eq_one`); the new proofs at `RegulatorLowerBounds.lean:48`, `:55`, `:74`, and `:78` are direct compositions of those lemmas.
findings.fix: Use `one_le_regulator_of_rank_eq_zero K (...)` / `regulator_eq_one_of_rank_eq_zero K (...)` at call sites, or restrict this PR to genuinely missing rank-zero criteria if those are needed.
findings.issue: The `card_infinitePlace`, `finrank`, `≤ 1`, and `< 2` regulator exact/lower-bound declarations are special cases of the existing rank-zero API, adding a parallel surface rather than new reusable mathematics.

summary: The new file adds only small special-case wrappers over the existing rank-zero regulator API and rank-zero criteria. This creates parallel regulator APIs where callers can already compose the existing lemmas directly.
